name: Holiday
description: Christmas, Thanksgiving, New Year's, Boxing Day — holidays around the world.

questions.q: What is the name of the day after Christmas, observed as a holiday in the UK and Commonwealth countries?

source.notes: December 26. Has nothing to do with the sport — historically tied to giving 'Christmas boxes' to servants.

questions.q: On what date do many Hispanic countries celebrate 'Día de Reyes' (Three Kings' Day)?

source.notes: Also known as the Feast of the Epiphany.

questions.q: On what date do Russian, Ukrainian, and many other Orthodox Christians celebrate Christmas?

source.notes: Orthodox churches still using the Julian calendar celebrate Christmas on January 7 (= December 25 Old Style).

questions.q: On which day of the year is Thanksgiving celebrated in the United States?

source.notes: Set by FDR and Congress in 1941 (previously the last Thursday).

questions.q: What bird is traditionally served as the centerpiece of Thanksgiving dinner?
What is the iconic Thanksgiving morning parade held in New York City since 1924?

source.notes: Originally called the Macy's Christmas Parade in 1924; renamed in 1927.

questions.q: What spiced dessert is the traditional finale to a Thanksgiving meal?
What name is given to the major shopping day immediately following Thanksgiving?
What is the name of the online-shopping holiday on the Monday after Thanksgiving?
What annual White House ceremony spares one or two birds from the Thanksgiving table?

source.notes: The National Thanksgiving Turkey Presentation. The pardon-style tradition began informally with JFK; formalized by George H.W. Bush in 1989.

questions.q: What is the first name of Charles Dickens' famously stingy character Scrooge in 'A Christmas Carol'?
Bing Crosby's recording of which Irving Berlin song is one of the best-selling singles ever?

source.notes: Crosby's 1942 recording is estimated to have sold over 50 million copies.

questions.q: Which 1965 TV special featured a melancholy jazz score by Vince Guaraldi and a sad little tree?
Which 1990 family film stars Macaulay Culkin defending his house from burglars during Christmas?

source.notes: Directed by Chris Columbus; written by John Hughes.

questions.q: In which U.S. state can you find a town named 'Santa Claus' (and a Santa Claus Post Office)?

source.notes: Santa Claus, IN. Receives hundreds of thousands of letters from kids each year.

questions.q: What year did the first Rockefeller Center Christmas tree go up in New York City?

source.notes: Construction workers put up an unadorned 20-foot tree during the Depression. Public lighting ceremony followed in 1933.

questions.q: What reindeer with a 'very shiny nose' was created in 1939 as a Montgomery Ward giveaway booklet?

source.notes: Created by Robert L. May; the song version came a decade later.

questions.q: What is 'Merry Christmas' in Spanish?
What festive shopping holiday on the Saturday after Thanksgiving promotes locally-owned stores?
On what date does New Year's Day fall under the Gregorian calendar?
